title: salix nivalis var. saximontana
intro: Salix nivalis var. saximontana, also known as Rocky Mountain willow or mountain cottonwood, is a variety of the Salix nivalis plant, which is a type of willow tree native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain and swelling. The health benefits of Salix nivalis var. saximontana include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of menopause. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves to harness its medicinal properties.
